assertion_results.text: Review identifies at least one SQL injection vulnerability as a critical or high severity security finding
assertion_results.evidence: Review mentions SQL injection but does not assign a severity level (critical/high/medium/low/info) to any finding

assertion_results.text: Review identifies the SQL injection in the search_users endpoint specifically mentioning string concatenation into the query
assertion_results.evidence: States 'The search_users function concatenates user input directly into the SQL query'

assertion_results.text: Review identifies the SQL injection in the delete_user endpoint
assertion_results.evidence: States 'Similarly, the delete_user function concatenates user_id into the query'

assertion_results.text: Each finding includes a severity level (critical, high, medium, low, or info)
assertion_results.evidence: No severity levels assigned to any of the 5 findings. Uses numbered list without severity classification.

assertion_results.text: Each finding includes a specific fix suggestion with code showing parameterized queries
assertion_results.evidence: Shows parameterized query code for search_users but not for delete_user. Partial pass — one code fix shown.

assertion_results.text: Findings are categorized by type (security, bug, performance, style, or maintainability)
assertion_results.evidence: Findings are in a flat numbered list without category labels. No categorization system used.

assertion_results.text: Review includes a summary section with overall assessment
assertion_results.evidence: Has a brief closing paragraph but no structured summary section with overall assessment rating

assertion_results.text: Review identifies the missing database connection cleanup (conn.close or context manager) as a resource leak
assertion_results.evidence: States 'database connections are never closed, which could lead to issues'
